beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee at home.
beats.reasoning: Low energy from night shifts means slow morning ritual; coffee at home before venturing out. Warm Friday sets up desire to get outside.

beats.activity: Drive to Pike Place Market for breakfast.
beats.reasoning: Aisha loves Pike Place on quiet mornings; 35 min drive from Columbia City across town. Friday morning before crowds peak.

beats.activity: Breakfast and people-watching at Pike Place Market.
beats.reasoning: Ritual from Day -1 energized her; settling, social without effort. Nice morning light, few tourists yet. 77F and breezy makes it pleasant.

beats.activity: Walk to Capitol Hill, browse boutiques and coffee shops.
beats.reasoning: 15 min walk downhill from Pike Place; warm weather invites wandering. Pride kickoff energy building but street closures don't start until 5pm, so early afternoon is clear.

beats.activity: Lunch at a new restaurant on Capitol Hill.
beats.reasoning: Aisha loves trying new spots and good sourdough; replicates the joy from Day -1 lunch. Lunchtime crowds manageable before Pride fair ramps up.

beats.activity: Continue browsing Broadway shops and thrift stores.
beats.reasoning: Warm, breezy weather perfect for window shopping. Pride energy visible but not disruptive yet. Social without commitment—fits her exhausted-but-social mood.
Heat building (77F) and Pride street closures starting at 5pm; smart to head home before downtown gets chaotic. 40 min drive avoids traffic.

beats.activity: Grocery shopping and meal prep at home.
beats.reasoning: 10 min walk from home; restocks after recent meals out. Warm day means hydration focus. Fits her self-care rhythm from recent days.

beats.activity: Return home, chop vegetables, prep meals for the week.
beats.reasoning: Autopilot chopping like Day -1 wind-down; kitchen ritual is grounding. Afternoon light through windows, true crime podcast playing—her happy place.

beats.activity: Order dinner in, eat, and wind down with true crime show.; Long soak in the bath with lavender bath bombs and tea.; Continue true crime binge-watching, wind down for bed.
beats.reasoning: Skips Pride downtown chaos (road closures, crowds); prefers home comfort. Exhaustion from night shifts + warm day = chill evening. Dinner delivery avoids cooking.
6.5 hours rest, ending before 06:00. Night-shift worker catching up on sleep debt; full recovery night before potentially returning to work rotation.

diary: Woke up to perfect weather and decided to treat myself before the shift rotation kicks in. Pike Place had that Friday energy—grabbed breakfast and just sat there watching the flower vendors and tourists blur together. The market always settles something in me. Walked over to Capitol Hill after, caught the pre-Pride vibes building on Broadway. Grabbed lunch at Serious Pie and browsed the thrift shops, trying on vintage jackets I didn't need. Headed home around lunch, did my usual grocery haul and prepped vegetables for the week—something about chopping and organizing makes the chaos feel manageable. By evening, the city was gearing up for the street fair, but I was already in my bath with lavender bombs and a true crime podcast. Felt good to have the day mostly to myself, quiet and intentional.
thought_process: I needed today to be slow after a stretch of doubles at Harborview. Knowing Pride was happening downtown made me want to get out early and soak in that energy without the crowds—Pike Place and Capitol Hill were my way of being part of the city's good mood without the intensity. The meal prep wasn't just practical; it's my weekly ritual that keeps me grounded. I skipped the evening street fair even though part of me wanted to join it, but after months of emergency room shifts, I craved stillness more than celebration. The bath and true crime became my version of self-care—something absorbing enough to quiet my mind but low-key enough to let me actually rest.
world_event_prompt: Warm, breezy Friday, high of 77F. Seattle Pride kickoff: Capitol Hill street fair and road closures along Broadway and Pike from 5pm. Festive, busy evening downtown.
